will be processed. However, this document doesn't provide any guidance on clinical and therapeutic

appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY and selection of
corresponding Health Benefit Package.

for hospitals to determine and submit the mandatory documents required
for claiming reimbursement of health benefit package under PMJAY.1.2 Clinical key pointers:a. Vaginal myomectomy is a minimally invasive surgery as an alternative to abdominal orlaparoscopic myomectomy. It is indicated in some specific cases where the submucous fibroid
is just protruding through the external Os and the pedicle is not accessible. Or polyp is sessile
or broad based.
b. The diagnosis made should be backed by clinical &/or USG findings and all required reports

Symptoms: Heavy menstrual bleeding/ irregular bleeding/ urinary pressuresymptoms/ heaviness and pain in abdomen/ dysmenorrhea/ mass in lower
abdomen/ infertility/ asymptomatic
ii. Physical examination:

to be performed.i. Symptoms: Heavy menstrual bleeding/ irregular bleeding/ urinary pressuresymptoms/ heaviness and pain in abdomen/ dysmenorrhea/ mass in lower
abdomen/ infertility/ asymptomatic
ii. Physical examination:1. General examination- Specially check for pallor
2. Abdominal examination- Check for any supra pubic mass or lump
3. Per speculum examination- Inspect Cervix for visibility of fibroid
4. Per vaginal examination- Assess for uterine size (enlarged and / or

inal examination- Check for any supra pubic mass or lump
3. Per speculum examination- Inspect Cervix for visibility of fibroid
4. Per vaginal examination- Assess for uterine size (enlarged and / orirregular uterus)
